steps.title: Notice Samuel's balanced traits
steps.content: The passage presents Samuel as daring without rashness, prompt without thoughtlessness, and firm without harshness. It then says he won both his officers' respect and his comrades' love. These details show a strong fit for military leadership.

steps.title: State the main idea
steps.content: Choice A overstates attention-seeking, Choice C invents mistakes and contentment, and Choice D invents independent actions outside orders. The main point is that Samuel's character makes him well suited to his military role. The answer is B.
